I just had my technical judgement interviews today for Google TPM new grad
They didn’t ask any system design (in a traditional sense) or coding questions

1 interview just asked 3 questions: explain a complex intern project you’ve worked on to a VP in the company, explain how you solved a technical problem in the past to a non-technical audience, & identify why technical/product compliance is important
The other tech interview had a couple of questions: explain any complex technical concept to a non-technical audience & a couple of high-level questions about how you’d design a system

Since I talked about ML as my complex technical concept, I designed a computer vision system (i.e. how to check if an x-ray scan shows a fracture) and a NLP system (i.e. how to make an all-lowercase paragraph have capital letters [according to English grammar conventions])
I also had to design a global identification system, so the user can provide any “username” and receive a unique “password” that’s specific to only that username
I haven’t posted my interview experience in #interview-experience yet as I haven’t finished my on-site interviews yet
Best of luck! Pretty similar to mine.

Is there anything i can do to speed up team match at Google? The wait is killing me, my anxiety has heightened😭
Have you had any team match calls yet?
Nope😭
Tell them you’d like to pay for GPremium
Whats GPremium?
how long has it been since you heard you were moving to team-match?
Nah it was a joke, to expedite the interview process, imagine you pay like a $7.99 one time fee
Do you have any competing offers?

If you haven't had one call, it may be worthwhile to sent your recruiter an updated resume that better highlights unique skillsets you have to offer. Keep in mind, in order to get team match calls, a manager looks at your resume and interviews feedback, if you arent hearing back from managers, chances you aren't providing what managers are looking for or interview feedbacks may not be as strong. It could also just be an issue of low headcount- which makes it competitive.
It could also be that you told recruiters your preferences which narrows down your options. I told my recruiter I was willing to work any office and didnt have a strong preferences in teams
A friend of mine got team matched for a PgM position after 6 months...because his area of interest was very specific

If you have connections at Google, ask then to find you roles that are open to new grads. And then send those links to the recruiter proactively.. alternatively, if you can get the HM information from your connection, ping them directly on LinkedIn?

Does anyone know here if Goldman Sachs offers relocation assistance?
I do know they give you a sign on bonus after passing your background check. It’s a sum between $7,500-$10,000. My assumption is that money should also help you with relocation needs
They do
Do you know how much? Is it taxed?
Thank you!

$14k (although could be more or less depending on the level). It's treated as an expense so company covers it even though tax is withheld when you cash out. You can cash out only 50% of what is remaining from the $14k
I am a new grad. So analyst. Would that be same?
Not sure about the amount but otherwise process should be the same
Im a little confused. So will I have to show them the expense report?
Do I not get like a lump sum?
What would be the best thing to do in this case

No you will not get a lump sum. A third party will manage your relocation and provide you with the $14k credit on their portal. Once you upload your  receipts eligible for relocation in on their portal they will subtract that amount from the $14k and reimburse you. After your relocation is complete and you added all eligible expenses you email them to cash out the remaining amount. They approve and will notify the company about the cash out (50% of the remaining amount), and you will get this in your bank account in a few pay cycles

The third party will reach out to you closer to your start date about your relocation plans and will explain to you what is covered in the relocation amount. They also assist you in booking tickets, finding temporary accommodation, and provide you with an real estate agent that can help find you a place/neighborhood for rent
Oh okay, so do I use less of the 14k so I can get more of 50%?

It's up to you. The max cash you get is $7,000 by directly cashing out. But if you use the relocation money you will end up with more value. Example: let's say you expense $2000 of your first month rent, $500 flight ticket and cash out. In this scenario you'll end up with $8,250 in value, which is $5,750 cash ($11,500 remaining / 2), $2500 in expenses reimbursed. Makes sense?
Once you cash out you cannot expense    anymore
Yes! I think im gonna do that

Which is better to choose as a new grad? A Java SWE (I’m about 6/10 in Java) or Data Engineer (I’m about 9/10 in Python) if I wanna have a career in AI in the long run? Java developer company is in fintech, but DE role is a well branded non tech company
(Not that I care about brand so much lol)
Need y’all’s take on this^^
IMO hands down DE.. Java SWE won't give that much experience/job satisfaction/compensation growth/flexibility to pivot to a different role - DA/DS
Any international students here have any idea if you can travel to your country when on OPT? I have my EAD card and am planning to go to my country before my job starts. Any risks in that?
@nixhyun If you don't get an answer here, your company should have a global mobility office and they may know the answer.
I havent yet started my job so idk who to ask the question to
Your recruiter should be able to point you in the right direction.

Yes you can. As long as you’ve got a Travel Permission Signature, a Valid Visa & Passport for at least 6 months after reentry, Evidence of employment and your EAD card then you should be good to renter the country and proceed with OPT
The Travel Signature should be valid too, i.e not older than 12 months from the date it was signed
Just a caution, see if your card has that “NOT VALID FOR REENTRY”, mine has it so I didn’t risk leaving. Don’t know if we can though
Thank you, do you think I’d be okay if I wanted to shift to SWE later? Say after a year or two?

You can travel on OPT, as long as you adhere to the requirements I highlighted above. The “Not Valid for Reentry” message means the card on its own can not be used as a stand alone document to reenter the US. It must be accompanied by supporting documents (mentioned above)
Oh okay, thanks for clarifying. This clears things up

I don’t know what to do
Check if they filed h1b visa before for your role/title: https://www.myvisajobs.com/H1B_Visa.aspx. If yes then they are likely to sponsor you as well

Question for international student grads:
You normally have a SEVIS profile where you update the employer info with their name, address and how it’s related to your major. If you put in an employers info but then took another offer later, can you just delete this employer’s info in the SEVIS OPT profile, and then upload the new employers info?

@boomboomyeah I am not sure if you can delete however what happens is that it will update  the new employer info and at the same time it is advisable to put down a one or two liner email with University DSO about the change
There is an option to delete, so I just wasn’t sure if the previous job was gonna count as employment

okay that is good if you can delete. I wasn't sure if you had already started working for the previous employer but if you had I believe it will show in your record if you worked for them even for a day even if you delete it.

Assuming your recruiter has not responded yet, have you considered reaching out to someone who already works there (someone you may already have connected to before either via LinkedIn, college, past colleague) to see if they can point you to another recruiter or even an HR point of contact?

I’ve been talking with an account executive. And basically, they’re a vendor who pay me when I work for another company. Basically, no one in my circle who work there wants the visa sponsorship. I was thinking of reaching out to a law firm to see what the process is so that I can guide my company on how to do it from an employer’s perspective. So sorry you are going through that :/ 
I am not familiar with H-1B visa filing, though at this point this sounds like a legal question and unfortunately I am not qualified to provide advice. I can try to do some research about it and can DM you with anything potentially useful I find.

You should get legal help.. it's going to be hard filing it yourself. A good law firm can guide your employer on what to do.

The filing deadline is Apr 1, right? You have enough time , provided the company has an immigration firm.. if they don't and need to onboard the lawfirm as a vendor, it might take some more time.
I thought it was Match 1. Wait, I think it’s the starting date

also take a look at this woman on LI who has a lot of info: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/soundarya-balasubramani_h1b-layoffs-layoff-activity-7023680133697863680-zoZV

If you've not been getting a lot of stories every sprint, say just one or two, does that indicate poor performance by you (potentially to not continue employment at that company), or not necessarily?
My contract ends and I'm a bit nervous about how to approach my manager about potentially turning full-time. Any tips would help greatly
New grad here, so not exactly sure if this is how SWE/PM life works and is normal
Can you clarify what you mean by not getting a lot of stories per sprint? Are you a swe?
For now, yes. I am interviewing for PM roles.
Am looking for mock interview.can someone please help by tomorrow

I would advise running a diagnostic yourself? 
1/ are the total number of stories for your team decreasing? 
2/ are other swes on the team feeling the same? 
3/ who assigns stories - have you chatted with them about your capacity? 
4/ has your direct manager given you any sense of your performance? 
5/ do other people know your contract is ending and could have assigned fewer stories?

I’m looking to fill a intern position for project manager with 6-12 months duration in Tesla’s Supply Chain team in Palo Alto, CA.

The person will:
- Develop detail project plans and track key activities for battery raw materials
- Prepare weekly dashboard on production status & quality
- Coordinate internal resources to ensure the completion of milestones
- Drive decision making and program goal alignment
- Support data visualization and reporting

The ideal candidate should at least have bachelor’s degree in engineering (mechanical, manufacturing, chemical, material, electrical), and is passionate about project management with a diverse team of engineers.

Please reach out if you or someone you know meets the criteria and is interested in the opportunity.

messages.content:
Hi all, I'm an incoming Microsoft TPM intern for summer 2025. I'll be graduating in 2026 and would love to learn more about the new grad job market for TPMs. 

I understand that the market it small, but if anyone knows which companies have these opportunities (outside of Msft, Tesla, Google, Amazon, maybe Meta?)  I would love to chat about it. 

Also if anyone has any tips for landing a new grad position or any tips in general I'm all ears and here to learn!! Already learned so much from lurking around here
You may want to look at links posted in the last few months in this channel to see which other companies are hiring at that level.
the other option is to look at adjacent roles : program managers, Ops PgM, CX PgM, etc. and then plan your transition to TPM once you've gained a few yrs exp
if you don't have immigration related constraints, checking out startups is also a good idea. they might look to hire "project managers" at entry levels

messages.content: Anyone have any good tips/resources for a "case" interview final round for Roblox APM? Would be much appreciated

Roblox APM (San Mateo) vs Microsoft TPM (Redmond), both New Grad Summer

Microsoft I would be returning to the same org, I liked the culture, work, and people. Roblox is a new venture for me. I prefer the bay over puget sound area. I am interested in gaming. Overall Roblox is getting a lot of bad press, I ask or tell anyone about Roblox and I get a negative reaction. 

I'm not scared of bad press (would work at Palantir or Anduril in a heart beat), but I realize Microsoft would be a safer bet than Roblox long term.

Never did product, mostly TPM internships. I am also working on a startup and Microsoft would give me the WLB to pursue it on the side Brian Armstrong style, but not too sure about Roblox.

Anyone have insight they would like to add?

All of my peers are saying Roblox is a no brainer, due to the "prestige", but I don't care about prestige. I just want to make an impact and work hard Tesla style.

Would be interested in what more senior people would say!

Feel free to take this with a grain of salt, but I would personally pick Roblox for a couple different reasons. For reference, I worked at AWS as a software engineer for a couple years, took a few years off to do other stuff, and am now transitioning into becoming a TPM.

The biggest thing for me would be the opportunity to work in Product and see if I enjoyed that vs. being a TPM, since you've already done multiple TPM internships. Especially with the rotational program aspect of being able to work on multiple teams and under different mentors, you'll get a couple different views on how PMs operate, which you can then use to decide whether you enjoy one role over the other.

I'm not entirely sure about this, but I feel it would be easier to switch back from PM -> TPM with your prior experience as opposed to the other way around.

The other reasons are location (since you prefer the Bay) and the fact that you enjoy gaming (it'll probably be easier to transition from Roblox to another gaming studio if that's something you're interested in down the line). 

The only reason I would go with Microsoft really boils down to how much you believe in your startup idea and if you want as much time as possible to work on it.

Thank you so much Yun! I agree I feel like PM to TPM is easier for me versus the other way around 100%.

I really appreciate this insight so much!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
